sentence_reviews.text: Document-intelligence systems extract entities, relations, tables, layouts, classifications, and structured records from text-heavy or scanned documents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: BERT/Transformer references support text extraction and classification foundations; structured-output references support records, and multimodal/OCR/layout parts are plausible synthesis rather than directly verified.

sentence_reviews.text: They bridge unstructured organizational evidence and transactional software while preserving traceable source context.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Layout variation, OCR error, schema drift, long documents, and ambiguous entities require validation and provenance.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Structured-output and transformer references support schema and extraction concerns; OCR/layout/provenance limitations are plausible synthesis but not all directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json terminal flag and current dependency graph consumer set.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is graph metadata: the node is terminal in the current atlas graph because no other canonical node currently depends on it.

sentence_reviews.text: Document intelligence & information extraction integrates Encoder-only bidirectional transformers as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: BERT supports encoder-only bidirectional transformers as core for text-heavy document understanding.

sentence_reviews.text: Document intelligence & information extraction integrates Hidden Markov models & CRFs as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: HMMs/CRFs are classical sequence labeling methods for extraction, but optional in modern systems.

sentence_reviews.text: Document intelligence & information extraction integrates Multimodal foundation models as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Scanned documents and layout extraction make multimodal foundation models a common dependency in synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Document intelligence & information extraction integrates Structured outputs & typed action generation as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Extraction into records directly supports structured-output dependencies.

sentence_reviews.text: Document intelligence & information extraction integrates Provenance, citations & evidence tracking as part of an end-to-end application boundary.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Packet candidate references and dependency rationale.
sentence_reviews.notes: Traceable source context and validation make provenance/citation tracking a common dependency.

edge_reviews.notes: BERT supports encoder-only bidirectional transformers as core for text-heavy document understanding.
HMMs/CRFs are classical sequence labeling methods for extraction, but optional in modern systems.
Scanned documents and layout extraction make multimodal foundation models a common dependency in synthesis.
Extraction into records directly supports structured-output dependencies.
Traceable source context and validation make provenance/citation tracking a common dependency.
